summary: Created a bounded second M7 UX recovery task graph for the systemic interaction blockers named by the product owner: missing obligation handling entry, unclear appointment preview/confirm feedback, lack of turn/phase/action rhythm, and weak Nobunaga-like strategic readability.

tests_run.command: git fetch origin --prune && git pull --ff-only origin main
tests_run.result: origin/main was already up to date before planning branch creation.
No READY task before planning because content lock was blocked pending product-owner decision.

decisions.decision: Downstream sequence is core action loop recovery, map decision-surface sync, then systemic interaction validation before content-lock reconsideration.
M7-EXIT-VALIDATION-001 must not start until the new recovery validation passes and content lock is explicitly reconsidered by the product owner.

risks.summary: Future fixes may again polish panels without closing command entry and feedback loops.
risks.mitigation: Acceptance criteria require playable flows, task-to-entry closure, and command-result feedback.

risks.summary: The feedback could be misread as final art direction.
risks.mitigation: Tasks explicitly allow low-fidelity UI and forbid production-art/content expansion scope.

requested_action: Read-only review of the new task graph, content-lock blocker state, allowed/forbidden paths, and absence of product-code changes.
